This accommodation is fully utilised unless, on occasion, a property is void for a short period of time between placements.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline how the provisions in the 2024-25 Budget and the 2025-26 Draft Budget support childcare.
answer_text: The Executive provided a ring-fenced allocation of £25 million to the Department of Education to support children, parents and providers with early learning and childcare in 2024-25.  This included the local Childcare Subsidy Scheme. Over 14,000 children have already registered and it is reducing family childcare bills by 15%, supporting hard working families by making childcare more affordable.  This was as a first step towards the development of a full Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.The Draft Budget for 2025-26 provides £50 million for the Childcare Strategy, doubling the amount available in 2024-25. That draft Budget is currently out to consultation. A final Budget will be brought to the Executive for agreement. The Department of Education has policy responsibility for childcare. Therefore, it will be for the Education Minister to bring forward proposals on how the funding provided in the Budget will be used to support childcare. I understand that as part of the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy package of measures, the Department for the Economy is leading on a scoping exercise to determine the business support requirements of the childcare sector, which in turn, will help to inform any appropriate measures to be implemented.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Childcare Subsidy Scheme will be extended beyond March 2025.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the to the answer to AQW 21472/22-27.The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 7 February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how she plans to develop tourism in Fermanagh.
answer_text: Question 7 AQO 1591/22-27My department’s newly launched Tourism Vision and Action Plan sets out a regional approach to growing tourism across the North in the next 10 years, benefitting all regions, including Fermanagh. Tourism NI will apply a destination approach to deliver inclusive, balanced sub-regional growth and help to address economic inactivity in both urban and rural communities. The planned inclusion of Fermanagh as part of the Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands brand, led by the All-Island Strategic Tourism Group, will deliver increased connectivity and an improved visitor experience across the Island. Bringing together the internationally renowned experiences that Ireland has to offer, will encourage international visitors to explore more widely, stay longer, and spend more. The Cuilcagh Lakelands Geopark proposals have also been identified as a priority investment within the Mid-South-West Growth Deal.  With five elements stretching from the Marble Arch Caves up to Castle Caldwell, the potential £21m package provides an opportunity to build on the work to date in creating a cross-border destination across Cavan and Fermanagh.  I look forward to speaking at the Fermanagh and Omagh Tourism Conference on 27th February to shine a spotlight on the benefits of regenerative tourism and encouraging current and future tourism initiatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline any work being taken forward by the Civil Service to support employees affected by domestic abuse.
answer_text: To support staff affected by domestic and sexual abuse, the Civil Service, as an employer, signed the White Ribbon Charter in 2019 and 2023, pledging to raise awareness of this issue and the importance of preventing its occurrence. Alongside this, the Civil Service has: implemented a Domestic and Sexual Abuse Policy and e-learning for staff in 2021; introduced support measures for staff who are victims, including 10 days paid special leave to facilitate practical arrangements, such as seeking advice or securing alternative accommodation; trained 69 staff as Safe Place Advocates and 600+ Department for Communities staff in “Supporting Customers affected by Domestic Abuse”; and delivered internal communications to all staff signposting to sources of support. In recognition of this work, the Civil Service attained the Platinum Workplace Charter Award by ONUS (a social enterprise offering training and consultancy services on domestic violence and abuse).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Defective Premises Act (Northern Ireland) 2024.
answer_text: The Defective Premises Act attained Royal Assent on 20 September 2024. The Act came into effect on 21st September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the steps he is taking to reinstate the funding to the (i) Fermanagh Classic Fishing Festival; and (ii) the Erne Pike Classic Festival.
answer_text: Budget pressures have required prioritisation of resources to deliver statutory obligations and the management of the Public Angling Estate. However, my department will continue to support the Fermanagh Classic Fishing Festival and the Erne Pike Classic by making the Public Angling Estate available for use.Officials have had constructive engagement with Fermanagh and Omagh District Council to enable support for the Fermanagh Classic Fishing Festival. The department will subsequently engage with the organisers of the Erne Pike Classic to agree arrangements.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the Transport Strategy 2035 will be published.
answer_text: The preparation of a new transport strategy is now at an advanced stage. This new strategy will inform the planning and delivery of transport infrastructure and services up to 2035.The Strategy will set out my vision for our transport network. In doing so, it will reaffirm and advance my Department’s commitment to place Cleaner and Greener connections, a key foundational objective of the Department, at the centre of the development of the transport network. The Strategy is about connecting communities, creating opportunities for all, and reaching our potential by facilitating a regionally connected economy that can capitalise on innovation and green growth opportunities.The Strategy will also outline how the Department is working to address the challenges we face such as congestion, climate emergency and transport safety.  In preparing the Strategy my officials have been listening to a wide range of voices, including those representing disabled people, the environmental sector, consumers and industry.I look forward to publishing my draft Strategy later this year and will welcome feedback on it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any plans he has to strengthen animal welfare legislation, particularly in relation to dog ownership laws.
answer_text: I am currently undertaking a series of meetings with key stakeholders, to determine priorities for reform of Northern Ireland’s animal welfare legislation in the remainder of this mandate. Reforms to improve the welfare of dogs, such as strengthening microchipping requirements are included in these discussions. Following this engagement, it is my intention to publish an animal welfare pathway which will set out animal welfare reforms which I wish to see delivered within the lifetime of this Assembly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on his work to reduce MOT waiting lists.
answer_text: In each of the last three years the Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) conducted over 1.1 million vehicle tests which are the highest numbers ever recorded. This increase in capacity has been achieved through a range of measures including the recruitment of additional vehicle examiners. Following a concerted recruitment campaign in 2024, the DVA recruited 35 new vehicle examiners.The DVA also use overtime to provide cover for leave and offer vehicle test appointments on Sundays and bank holidays when testing is not normally available. The DVA release vehicle test appointments on a daily basis, when resources become available, so customers are encouraged to regularly check the booking system as they may be able to change their existing appointment to a different test centre and time if a slot becomes available.  In 2024, there were only six days in the year when the DVA did not offer MOT appointments. Disappointingly, on average around 800 vehicle test appointments are missed each week due to no shows, placing additional demand on this service.In June 2024, I introduced Temporary Exemption Certificates for five- and seven-year-old cars within specified date ranges.  This measure, along with the high number of vehicle tests conducted, means the average waiting time for an MOT test has fallen from 99 days on 1 June 2024 to 30 days on 13 January 2025.  To further increase vehicle testing capacity, the DVA is investing in new test centres. It is anticipated that two new test centres at Hydebank and Mallusk will open for vehicle testing in 2025. Once fully operational, these new centres will provide the capacity to test over 200,000 additional vehicles per annum, which will help meet the future demand for this service.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the redevelopment of Enniskillen Library.
answer_text: A new public library facility at Enniskillen remains a priority for my Department and Libraries NI.  An addendum to the original business case for the library has been approved by the Department of Finance subject to the availability of capital funding. The future contribution of the Department to the project in 2025/26 will be considered alongside the Department’s other priority capital projects.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are any further developments on the prospect of introducing legislation to prohibit the overtaking of a stationary school bus.
answer_text: I have repeatedly voiced my concern at the number of deaths and serious injuries on our roads. However, the evidence is clear that the key to enhanced road safety is improved driver and road user behaviour is key to improved road safety. It is essential that drivers should exercise due care and attention on our roads, at all times. With regard to school buses, the Highway Code already emphasises the need to drive slowly and carefully around schools and when passing a stationary bus carrying school children. I have no current plans to introduce a statutory prohibition on overtaking school buses.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the meals on wheels service in Fermanagh and South Tyrone.
To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 17223/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Education for a timeline of the repair works at Erne Integrated College, Enniskillen.
answer_text: I can advise that a contractor has been appointed to undertake the repair works at Erne Integrated College, Enniskillen.  The work is programmed to commence in late November 2024 and complete in February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to approve the use of Apple AirPods Pro 2, which has an inbuilt clinical quality hearing aid facility, to allow people with hearing loss to upgrade their devices.
answer_text: In the UK, the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) regulates medical devices ensuring they are safe and work as intended.  Hearing devices / aids are Class IIa medical devices and are regulated by the MHRA.In Northern Ireland, medical devices provided as part of an audiological assessment are specifically programmed for an individual patient’s need and are procured under an NHS national framework.Over the counter amplification devices can be purchased, of which Apple AirPods Pro 2 would be such device.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) whether the Meals on Wheels service is still being provided; (ii) the number of recipients in Fermanagh, broken down by district electoral area; and (iii) the cost per meal.
answer_text: Meals on wheels service is still provided in this Trust areaThe Trust has indicated that there are 6 people living in their own homes in the Belleek area in Fermanagh that receive meals on wheels 5 days per week.  Meals on wheels are also provided to 4 day centres, (Kesh, Killadeas, Drumahaw & Tir Navar), 5 days per week and the number of people who receive these varies according to attendances on any given day.The cost to the individual is £1.50 per meal.  The Trust subsidies these meals and the cost per meal to the Trust varies from £2.99 to £9.48 per meal, depending on location and delivery costs per day.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) why the TNC2024/2 workload agreement is not being implemented; and (ii) any plan he has to implement it.
answer_text: The Teachers’ Workload Agreement, TNC 2024/2 has been agreed and was issued to all schools by the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) in April 2024. It recognises the importance of the role of the professional Teacher in ensuring that the educational needs of young people are met as fully as possible.It is the responsibility of Boards of Governors to ensure that the workload of all Principals, Vice Principals and Teachers is managed fairly and consistently to ensure that all teaching professionals can discharge their roles and responsibilities effectively for the benefit of the teaching and learning of pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what engagement his Department is having with the local community in relation to the Fermanagh and Omagh Sub Regional Transport Plan 2035.
answer_text: In developing the new suite of Transport Plans I recognise the importance, and value of gleaning knowledge and opinions from the local communities.My Officials undertook a public engagement exercise in January, covering the initial development stages of the Fermanagh and Omagh Transport Plan.This consisted of:young people engagement sessions at primary and secondary schools,public drop-in sessions at the Connect Centre, Omagh and at Enniskillen Library, an online meeting for Community and Voluntary Sector representatives; andan online survey on the NICS engagement platform, Citizen Space.A total 102 responses were received to the public engagement exercise. A significant majority were supportive of the project’s draft vision and objectives. A report on the findings has been published on the project webpage. Subsequently, my officials held an update session for the Community and Voluntary Sector on the 15th August 2024 and I am pleased to advise that this proactive engagement will continue as the project progresses.These early engagement exercises as we develop the draft Transport Plan should not be confused with the formal consultation which is anticipated early 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any action he is taking to address the issues in the delay of paying teachers' pensions.
answer_text: My Department is in communication with all pension scheme members who are awaiting implementation of their retirement pension in order to keep them informed of progress.The Department has authorised use of overtime to ensure that any remaining cases are expedited for payment as soon as the scheme member’s preferred choice of pension option is received.A number of payments have been delayed due to a software issue, which is being addressed urgently by the pension system provider.This has been the first year of an updated pension process involving member choice of options. I have asked relevant officials to carry out a review of all processes and timescales involved in the retirement process to ensure lessons learned this year are acted on in future years to ensure timely payments to retiring teachers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans he has to introduce a vaginal mesh compensation scheme.
answer_text: The Patient Safety Commissioner for England, Dr Henrietta Hughes, made recommendations on redress for those who were harmed by Sodium Valproate and Pelvic Mesh implants in the “Hughes Report”, which was published on 7 February 2024. Whilst Dr Hughes’ remit was specifically for England, it is acknowledged that patients from across the UK have been affected by these issues.  Financial redress for Sodium Valproate and Pelvic Mesh remains a reserved matter and the three Devolved Administrations are awaiting consideration and assessment from the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC). The UK Government have not yet taken a decision on redress. The Department continues to give careful consideration to the findings and recommendations within the Hughes Report, including continued engagement at official level with counterparts in England, Scotland and Wales. I have recently written to Baroness Merron, Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Patient Safety, Women’s Health and Mental Health at the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC), requesting an update on the consideration of the Hughes Report recommendations and have sought assurances that consultation with the Devolved Administrations will take place in a timely manner and in advance of any UK Government decisions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the consultation arising from the report on mother and baby institutions, Magdalene Laundries and workhouses.
answer_text: The 12-week consultation launched on 27th June 2024. This marked an important step forward as the views shared with us will help shape the legislation necessary to set up the Inquiry and Redress Scheme. We are grateful to all those who have contributed so far and would encourage those who have not yet had the opportunity to respond to do so before September 19th deadline. There have been fifteen online and face-to-face events including events in Belfast, Omagh, Newry and Derry/Londonderry. There has also been a number of events specifically for victim and survivor groups.The public consultation has been widely advertised locally and internationally including in 96 newspapers, with almost 70 responses to date.These events have also provided an opportunity to highlight other parts of the programme including support services, which over 300 people have accessed, and to encourage people to provide their oral testimony to the Independent Panel. Once the public consultation closes, we will aim to publish an analysis of the responses in October. We will then consider decisions that need to be taken with our Executive Colleagues with a view to bringing legislation into the Assembly as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment of waiting times for treatment at the Brackenburn Clinic; and (ii) what plans his Department has to address delays.
answer_text: (i) The mean waiting time for treatment at the Brackenburn Clinic is 2 years 7 months with the longest wait 6 years 4 months. There are currently 845 on the waiting list.  My Department acknowledges that waiting lists are unacceptably high.(ii) My officials have been working with the Gender Identity Services Pathway Review Group and the Gender Identity Services Liaison Panel to develop a solution which meets the immediate needs of service users and enables the service to expand services and build capacity over a period of time.  This solution would also address the long waiting times for this service. However, given the very significant funding challenges faced by my Department and the lack of an agreed budget it has not been possible to progress this work as we would have liked.  My officials are currently working with members of the Gender Identity Review Group and Gender Identity Liaison Panel to develop a business case to enhance existing service provision and will be delivered as part of normal service planning. Funding of around £660k in 2024/25 will be required recurrently to fund the service development.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the rationale for the decision to place Education Authority REACH workers on protective notice.
answer_text: Funding to the Education Authority to deliver the REACH programme has been provided under the joint DE and DOH Emotional Health and Wellbeing Framework supported by a three-year Business Case which ends on the 31 March 2024.  With regard to future funding, departments have not yet been advised of budget allocations for 2024/25.  Uncertainty on the future funding position has significant implications for projects reliant on earmarked funds, such as the REACH Programme.  In this context, the Education Authority has assessed the risks associated with the employment contracts for staff employed through these projects, beyond the date when current funding ends.  Unfortunately, this means that those staff on fixed term contracts, without a substantive post with the EA, have been placed within the statutory dismissal process in contemplation of the expiry of their fixed term contract of employment.  Emotional health and wellbeing is a priority for my Department and I am conscious of the benefits that the REACH programme has brought for our children and young people, however, it requires funding.  I have asked my officials to continue to liaise with EA Youth Services on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will be providing additional funding to the Western Health and Social Care Trust to allow their external domiciliary care agencies to meet the increase in the minimum wage in April 2024.
answer_text: The hourly rate paid to independent sector providers of domiciliary care is an agreed regional rate, which is reviewed annually in line with normal processes.Whilst my Department has no say in the rates that independent sector providers pay staff, work is ongoing to consider the current rate paid to providers for 2024/25.This includes consideration of what extent an uplift could be applied, in order to meet the challenges of the increased cost of provision (including increases in the minimum wage), and to allow providers to increase the rate paid to workers, and to improve terms and conditions at their own discretion. There is undoubtedly a need to support independent sector providers, in light of increasing costs for day to day operations, however this must also be balanced within the context of a challenging financial environment.We are engaging employers, unions and other sectoral partners through the Social Care Collaborative Forum and Social Care Fair Work Forum to address a wide range of issues relating to pay, terms and conditions and career progression across the social care sector.	However, I expect the financial position for my Department will remain very challenging and difficult choices will need to be made on what can be delivered within the funding available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the processes in place to support people who experience workplace bullying in the NI Civil Service.
answer_text: The Civil Service is committed to creating and sustaining an inclusive workplace where everyone is treated with respect and dignity, free from any form of inappropriate behaviour.  The Dignity at Work policy sets out types of inappropriate and unacceptable behaviour, the informal and formal procedures available to deal with such behaviour, including bullying, and highlights the sources of information and assistance available to staff. To support colleagues’ understanding and awareness of the types of behaviours expected in the Civil Service mandatory e-learning is provided to all staff on their individual responsibilities to create an inclusive workplace and to recognise bullying and harassment in the workplace.The range of support services and the types of support available to staff who may be experiencing bullying are outlined below:Support ServiceSupport ProvidedLine managementFirst point of contact for assistance and informal resolution. Dignity at Work advisors, NICSHRAdvice, guidance and assistance.NICS Mediation ServiceConfidential, in-house service which seeks to provide an informal and speedy resolution to workplace conflict.Trade Unions Advice, guidance, and representation.Welfare Support ServiceIndependent and confidential service, providing emotional and practical advice to staff.Occupational Health ServiceOccupational health and medical advice.Departmental Harassment Contact OfficersInformation, guidance, and advocacy.Employee Assistance Programme (Inspire)Range of wellbeing support, including confidential counselling service available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year.HR Service provider (HRConnect)Advice and guidance on the policy and procedure. Provides updates on the progress of formal complaints.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what actions he is taking to mitigate against any inconveniences and economic losses faced by horse traders due to Irish sea border infrastructure.
answer_text: As required by Article 49 (3) of Regulation (EU) 2017/625 (Official Controls Regulation), DAERA has robust procedures in place to prioritise the delivery of checks on live animals, including horses, being transported from Great Britain to minimise delays and associated impact on journey times. The UK Government has established the Movement Assistance Scheme (MAS) to provide financial support to traders wishing to move live animals and animal products from Great Britain to Northern Ireland.  Under the MAS, Official Veterinarian equine certification costs up to £500 excluding VAT are reimbursed. Further information on the MAS is available from the UK Government website via the link below: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/movement-assistance-scheme-get-help-with-moving-agrifood-goods-to-northern-ireland
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his plans to address the lack of adult ADHD assessment and services.
answer_text: Although there are currently no commissioned services for Adult Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), provision has grown organically across Trusts in response to population demand and expertise within HSC teams. While this has ensured a level of provision is available, the Department is aware that this has also led to inconsistencies between Trusts including pathways and gaps between what can be provided by Trusts in terms of demand and the level of prevalence of adult ADHD, leading to individuals waiting longer than intended for assessment and support.The Health and Social Care Trusts are committed to work closely and collaboratively with commissioners to develop innovative, cost effective, co-produced, quality, and evidenced based services that can meet the needs of patients with ADHD within Trust areas. This work would include the development of clinical pathways.DoH Strategic Planning & Performance Group (SPPG), alongside policy colleagues in the Department, are keeping the situation regarding the commissioning of Adult ADHD services under review, recognising that the demand for ADHD diagnosis has risen in recent years.  In line with the usual process for commissioning of services, any decision to commission Adult ADHD services in the future will be based on an assessment of the level of demand for services, and in the context of future budget availability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the housing development at Grosvenor Barracks, Enniskillen.
answer_text: My Department applied to Fermanagh & Omagh District Council in September 2023 to renew outline planning permission for 200 housing units on the Grosvenor site in Enniskillen.  The outline planning renewal is subject to the conditions and the signing of the Section 76 Planning Agreement for Affordable Housing which is currently being drafted.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the redevelopment for Enniskillen Royal Grammar School, Enniskillen.
answer_text: Enniskillen Royal Grammar School was announced to advance in planning under the Major Capital Works Programme in January 2013.The final technical design and pre-tender estimate work for the new school building is currently being undertaken by the appointed Integrated Consultant Team.  This work is anticipated to complete in July 2024.A planning application for the new build project was submitted in March 2023, however no decision on the application has been taken at this time.  .For this project to progress beyond the current design stage additional capital funding, for which I will be bidding, will be required to enable progression of this and other capital projects which are currently at a stand still.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the redevelopment for Holy Trinity Primary School, Enniskillen.
answer_text: Holy Trinity Primary School was announced as one of nine major capital works projects to advance in planning by the then Minister of Education on 23 June 2020.An Integrated Consultant Team (ICT) was appointed in November 2021, who will be responsible for the full design of the project and management of the building contractor. A Business Case was approved in June 2023 confirming the preferred option as a new build twenty-one class base primary school and nursery unit on a new site located east of the Irvinestown Road, Enniskillen. The project is currently at the Concept Design Stage (RIBA Stage 2), and a Report on this stage is anticipated to be lodged with the Department in March 2024.     Although still at an early stage and subject to change, current estimates are that construction work would commence on site in January 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the redevelopment of Enniskillen library.
answer_text: A new public library facility at Enniskillen remains a priority for my Department and Libraries NI.  Libraries NI will be required to submit a refreshed business case for the replacement of Enniskillen Library to the Department for approval.  Any decision to refresh the business case and progress the project through to construction stage must take account of the Department’s capital budget position for 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on plans to reintroduce emergency general surgery at the South West Acute Hospital.
To ask the Minister of Health how he will increase the number of NHS dentists in Fermanagh.
answer_text: A key part of the access issues facing patients are the rates being paid to our dentists for treatment.  The absence of Executive Ministers has meant that it has not been possible to progress the recommended pay uplifts for staffing groups for 2023/24.  This is now my immediate priority, and I am meeting with Trade Unions in early course to take this forward. Officials have already been making arrangements in anticipation of funding being made available to ensure that uplifts can be implemented as quickly as possible.I know that an additional £95m has been invested in General Dental Services via the Financial Support Scheme and Rebuilding Support Scheme since April 2020. This came to an end in July 2023 due to the severe financial pressures arising from the Department’s 2023/24 budget settlement.A pilot scheme is currently running to provide appointments to unregistered patients who require emergency dental treatment during the working week. If a patient is not registered with a dentist, requires emergency dental treatment, and has been unable to access an appointment, they can contact their local Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) office for advice.I am also conscious of the progress that has been made in the rest of the UK on dental reform and I will be discussing these issues with officials as soon as possible, to ensure that the sector here in NI is supported in delivering important care to patients. More immediately, officials have been developing investment proposals to further support General Dental Services and I will consider them in early course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 4560/22-27, whether she will provide schools, who are running on a deficit, with finances to help them replace the computers they are losing.
answer_text: Education Authority (EA) C2k in consultation with schools removed a number of devices at the end of their useful life in order to support security across the schools estate. C2k worked alongside each individual school to ascertain the impact of dropping devices from the network, due to the fact that they are at the end of their useful life and a security vulnerability in terms of potential data loss.  The decision as to which machines need to be removed was discussed with schools at an individual school level and the removal of these additional devices had no impact on the core number of devices schools have under the current managed service.The removal of these legacy machines was necessary due to the fact that they were left behind in schools following the last procurement and rollout of managed service devices for school.  However, the Department has committed funding through the Education Information Solutions (EdIS) Programme to replace the core devices when the next refresh is due. Additional monies over and above the planned rollout of managed service devices would be required in order to replace these legacy devices.  This is currently outside of the EdIS programme in terms of budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, given that her Department does not back pay successful applications for free school meals and uniforms to the time the application was received, what assessment has she made of the four week target to complete the processing of applications remaining in place.
answer_text: The passage of time since these questions were originally asked means that the issues raised are no longer relevant.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what his Department is doing for frontline health care workers who test positive for Covid-19 and have to take off work, yet are only getting statutory sick pay.
answer_text: On 24 September 2022 the Coronavirus Act 2020 (Extension of Powers to Act for the Protection of Public Health) Order (Northern Ireland) 2022 expired. Consistent with NHS England, NHS Scotland and NHS Wales, my Department notified HSC Employers that Terms & Conditions associated with COVID-19 absence were to be withdrawn from 1st October 2022, seeing a return to normal contractual arrangements. All staff employed in HSC have contractual rights to paid sickness absence of up to 6 months' full pay and 6 months’ half pay.

answer_text: The passage of time since these questions were originally asked means that the issues raised are no longer relevant.

answer_text: The passage of time since these questions were originally asked means that the issues raised are no longer relevant.

answer_text: The passage of time since these questions were originally asked means that the issues raised are no longer relevant.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what she is doing to assist schools who face potentially losing the use of their computers due to Dell/C2K software changes.
answer_text: C2k is working alongside each individual school to ascertain the impact of dropping devices from the network due to the fact that they are end of life and a security vulnerability in terms of potential data loss.  The decision as to which machines need to be removed will be discussed with schools at an individual school level.The removal of these additional devices will have no impact on the core number of devices schools have under the current managed service. Furthermore, C2k will be supporting the development of an improved device to pupil ratio under the Education Information Solutions (EdIS) Programme, which will replace the Education Network NI (EN(ni)) managed service contract over the coming year.  This will ensure that the impact of the removal of these additional devices is reduced.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he can intervene with the Driver and Vehicle Agency in the event that they fine people for not having valid vehicle tax, whilst waiting for their MOT appointment.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) work very closely with the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) on a range of common issues to try and ensure the best service possible is provided to the people here. However, Vehicle Excise Duty (Motor Tax) is an excepted matter that is administered by the DVLA in Swansea on behalf of HM Treasury. The DVA has no authority or influence with the DVLA on these matters. MLAs, acting on behalf of constituents, can contact the DVLA directly through a dedicated email address at DVLAMinisterials@dvla.gov.uk. The DVA has, however, made provision for customers who require an MOT to tax their vehicle and cannot secure an MOT appointment before their tax expires. Their advice is to:book the earliest available appointment; andkeep checking the booking system for an earlier appointment.If they get to within five days of the expiry date of their vehicle tax and they have been unable to secure an MOT appointment, or their vehicle tax has expired, they should contact the DVA Customer Services team at dva.customerservices@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk, and they will do their best to get an urgent appointment for the vehicle.  To date, all customers who have contacted the DVA in these circumstances have been offered an earlier appointment slot.For the latest information on MOTs I would ask you to encourage your constituents to visit NI Direct at Book, view, change or cancel an MOT/ vehicle test | nidirect.  This information is also included as an advice note with every MOT Reminder Letter issued to DVA customers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many full time NI Fire and Rescue Service crews were standing by in Enniskillen Station in the last 12 months.
answer_text: During the last 12* months there has been a total of 29 occasions when full time fire crews stood by at Enniskillen Fire Station to support the NIFRS regional response model. There was also another 25 occasions during this time period when On-Call crews from other Fire Stations were sent to standby at Enniskillen to support this response model.*Data from 1/10/21 - 30/9/22

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what support he will provide to those suffering with long term health conditions, who rely heavily on their electricity supply.
To ask the Minister of Education, in light of the issues with the NI Supply Teacher Register, when will supply teachers be paid.
answer_text: There was a delay of one week to the planned go-live date for the new NI Supply Teacher Register (NISTR) until 29 August due to difficulties encountered in migrating the data from old to new system.  Unforeseen technical issues were experienced on 29 and 30 August resulting in timeouts and system downtime.  The supplier moved rapidly to triple the capacity of the system overnight to ensure performance was no longer impacted by user volume.  However, schools’ inability to access to the system for the majority of August necessitated the manual input of thousands of retrospective bookings by the NISTR Support Team on schools’ behalf.   Whilst most supply teachers were paid as normal through the temporary payroll on the 12th working day of September for days worked through NISTR in August, it is regrettable that a proportion of this valuable workforce did not receive timely payment.  There were several reasons for this for example, bookings that were not accepted or approved, bank details not provided, technical access issues and coding and duplication errors within the transferred file to the Department.  There were also several hundred teachers already working on permanent or fixed-term contracts paid through the permanent payroll and therefore payment for NISTR days was included in their pay cheque on the last working day of the month.The Department and the NISTR Support Team are working intensively to ensure that salary payment for all approved bookings for September days (and any outstanding days from August) will be paid through the relevant payroll this month.It is normal for any new system to experience some bedding-in issues asusers find their way around the system and bugs are identified. As the EA Project Team has become aware of ‘gremlins’ in the new system they have worked diligently with the suppliers to resolve as expediently as possible. For example, initial difficulties with logins, signing-off bookings and selecting special schools for work were resolved by the end of week one. Workarounds have also been put in place to address other issues until the system functionality can be further enhanced.A number of fixes have been applied to provide additional functionality and address identified bugs and the new system is now functioning adequately.  Work continues to make system improvements based on user feedback and additional features will continue to be added throughout the developmental phase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education why planned training for users of the NI Supply Teacher Register was cancelled at the last minute.
answer_text: This question relates to events in August 2022.A new and improved Northern Ireland Supply Teacher Register (NISTR) was procured by the Education Authority (EA) which administers the system, and it went live on 29 August 2022 after a one week delay due to data migration issues.Originally training was to take place from 22 to 26 August 2022 but with the delay in go live date, training was rescheduled for 30 and 31 August 2022 with a view to schools being able to train on the live platform.NISTR is accepted as the only system through which schools can engage supply teachers in Northern Ireland. The EA continues to monitor activities through NISTR.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how her Department will ensure there is no repeat of the delays in issuing free school meals and school uniform grants.
answer_text: In recognising the importance of pupils’ Free School Meal and Uniform Grant entitlement being confirmed before the start of the academic year, the EA has undertaken extensive communications explaining to families that in order to ensure they were able access these supports from the start of the academic year, they should apply before 31 July. This was communicated via social media, the EA website and through letters issued to applicants who received support last year encouraging them to apply again for the current school year.  While many parents did apply early enough to ensure entitlement was in place from the start of the academic year, unfortunately this was not the case for all applications. The EA continues to make every effort to process applications as quickly as possible and has consistently met its target of processing 95% of free school meals and uniform grant applications within four weeks. As at 14 October, the EA was processing applications received on 16th September.In order to support parents in their applications, the EA provides extensive guidance materials and technical support. When an application is submitted without the appropriate documentation, all such applicants are sent a tailored email response explaining the correct documentation that they are required to provide. It is necessary to verify applicants’ eligibility in order to ensure that public funds are used correctly.EA is in the process of gathering information for a post project review of the current year’s application process and any lessons learned will be considered in determining how support could be improved for the next application cycle.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the urgent review of the areas of youth services and tuition, in the context of the abuse of trust offences, which was due to take place before the end of the last mandate.
answer_text: I remain committed to reviewing the scope of abuse of position of trust offences as I had promised to members during passage of the then Justice (Sexual Offences & Trafficking Victims) Bill.  This review is focussing on the capture of any evidence that there may be to support the extension of the abuse of position of trust offences to the specific areas of tuition and youth activities, where I know some members had expressed particular concern.   As part of the review, my Department intends to issue a call for evidence over the coming weeks.  It will be encouraging as wide a response as possible, including from those with particular experience of working with young people in the specific settings and from those young people who would be affected by any further legislative change.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to list the housing estates for which there is a current road or sewer bond outstanding.
answer_text: A list of unadopted roads for which bonds exist, arranged by Council areas, has been compiled.  This has been placed in the Assembly library for ease of reference.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to list all the unadopted housing developments.
answer_text: A list of unadopted roads for which bonds exist, arranged by Council areas, has been compiled.  This has been placed in the Assembly library for ease of reference.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans he has to deal with a reducing number of Dental practices prepared to treat NHS patients.
answer_text: Efforts to address these pressures have focussed on increasing capacity within practices, with the Rebuilding Support Scheme (RSS), recently extended until the end of 2022/23, offering enhanced payments in return for increasing levels of activity.  In addition to the £76m provided via the Financial Support Scheme in the last two financial years, the RSS is projected to invest an additional £6.9m by the end of 2022/23.  A condition of the RSS is that patients are treated on the basis of clinical need, rather than registration status.  This helps to ensure that those in most need of treatment are prioritised.To provide treatment for patients with urgent dental need, Emergency Dental Clinics are also available at weekends and Bank Holidays where the patient will be triaged remotely and appropriate advice given or treatment arranged.In addition, my Department intends to commence a scheme on 3 October 2022 throughout Northern Ireland to provide access to non-registered patients who are experiencing an emergency or urgent dental condition.Support being provided to dental practices will continue to remain under ongoing review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what advice is given to NHS patients who have been removed from their own Dentist as non paying patients and there are no other dentists prepared to take them on.
answer_text: Any General Dental Practitioner signed up to the Rebuilding Support Scheme is required to treat patients based on clinical need, regardless of their registration status. The Department’s advice to patients not currently registered with a practice is that they contact other practices to enquire if any have capacity to see them.In order to increase activity in General Dental Services, I introduced the Rebuilding Support Scheme (RSS), which rewards dentists based on treatments being provided.  The RSS has seen an increase in activity levels of around 20% over the first 5 months of 2022/23 and continues until the end of the financial year.Emergency Dental Clinics are also available at weekends and Bank Holidays where the patient will be triaged remotely and appropriate advice given or treatment arranged.My Department commenced a new initiative on 3 October 2022 throughout Northern Ireland to provide access to non-registered patients who are experiencing an emergency or urgent dental condition.  This will help ensure that irrespective of a patients’ registration status, appropriate treatment is available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the Enniskillen Driving and Vehicle Test Centre will reopen to the public.
answer_text: Prior to the pandemic, the use of the Driver and Vehicle Agency’s (DVA) online booking channel had been steadily increasing, making the provision of a counter service across 15 test centres an increasingly expensive service to operate with a reducing number of transactions being carried out. As a result, DVA had plans to phase out counter services and this approach is reflected in the design of the new test centre at Hydebank, and the new booking system, which do not support counter services.From March 2020, due to Covid-19, counter services have been closed in order to reduce the number of people within the test centres. The new booking system which was introduced in September 2021 offers more functionality than the old one, and future phases will expand this functionality further.  From 20 September 2021 to 18 June 2022, the online channel was used by 93% of customers for vehicle test bookings and 98% of customers for driver test bookings. Those remaining DVA customers who could not or did not wish to access the online service used the NI Direct call centre to make a booking.The DVA also conducted a pilot exercise with Digital Shared Services from DoF and Libraries NI in March 2022, whereby on designated days in targeted libraries in the Fermanagh and Omagh District Council area, DVA staff were in attendance to assist customers make an online booking. Whilst there was limited take up of this service the DVA is keen to continue to work with Libraries NI to promote the use of their PCs and WiFi for booking an MOT appointment.The DVA is currently reviewing its Health and Safety Risk Assessments with respect to all vehicle and driver testing services to ensure that these continue to be in keeping with the latest Covid-19 guidance issued by the Health and Safety Executive NI (HSENI). Any changes to the current testing processes will be communicated to customers through NI Direct and social media channels.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the timeframe between the Department receiving the Kantar report on price data and it being publicly released.
answer_text: From the Office of the Minister 	Gordon Lyons MLA								The response to AQW 1422/22-27 provides the timeline for the publication of the Kantar data. This information was received from Kantar at the end of January 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how much funding the Department provided to Kantar to commission the research on comparative price data between GB and the north.
answer_text: From the Office of the Minister 	Gordon Lyons MLA											The Department for the Economy does not provide funding to the retail analysts Kantar.		The Department purchases grocery category prices for Northern Ireland and Great Britain produced by Kantar. 	 	The cost of this information is considered to be commercially sensitive and therefore it is not available for publication.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to allow the application process for a bus pass to be completed online.
answer_text: I recognise the value placed on concessionary travel by those groups who benefit from it and I am keen to explore how the application and renewal processes can be improved.  Among the options to improve how my Department and Translink interact with customers that are being actively considered, is the option to apply for a SmartPass online.Discussions are ongoing with Translink around the infrastructure necessary to facilitate online application and these discussion are currently at an early stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the rising cost of fuel, whether he will increase the mileage allowance for domiciliary care workers.
answer_text: In light of the recent significant increases in fuel costs, my Department, Employers and Trade Unions have been involved in national and local discussions regarding a potential review of the current Agenda for Change (AfC) Mileage Rates.The national discussions have been led through the NHS Staff Council and have been exploring the options in relation to mileage reimbursement rates. Unfortunately it has not been possible to conclude a national agreement on an increase in fuel reimbursement rates.In light of no nationally agreed position, temporary increases have been agreed in Scotland and Wales until the end of July and June respectively. At the time of those agreements, no decision could be made locally due to election period restrictions. My Department is, however, considering options for Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she has any plans to extend the Free School Meals scheme to all children.
answer_text: The Department has commenced a Review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria.  The review is considering a number of options for free school meals and uniform grants, including the options for universal free school meal provision for certain year groups or all pupils and the removal/adjustment to current thresholds for Universal Credit/Working Tax Credit as well as the potential to legislate on uniform policy.   My Department continues to work to develop costed options and a consultation exercise will take place over this Autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy why the Kantar report on price data for GB and the north was published in May 2022, when the data had analysed price data from December 2021.
answer_text: At the beginning of May 2022, there was interest in Kantar price data as it related to the December 2021 period.  In order to ensure that the latest data for that period were available to all in an accessible manner the Department published a suite of figures for Northern Ireland and Great Britain on 10 May 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what is the policy for the flying of the Union flag on school premises.
answer_text: Flying of flags on school premises is a matter for each individual school Board of Governors (BoG) and their managing authority e.g. the Education Authority (EA) for controlled schools and Council for Catholic Maintained Schools (CCMS) for catholic maintained schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the Law Commission's recommendation that there should be a new offence created to target intentional encouragement or assistance of self-harm.
answer_text: In the Modernising Communications Offences report, published in July 2021 the Law Commission put forward a number of recommendations to address the harm arising from online abuse, one of which relates to the encouragement of glorification of serious self-harm at a high threshold. The Report recognises the importance of ensuring that any offence does not unduly criminalise vulnerable people. The UK Government is currently considering that recommendation and the details of such a new offence.The Department of Digital, Culture, Media and Sport (DCMS) is leading on the Online Safety Bill and it is my understanding that an amendment to the Bill to add an offence of encouraging or assisting self-harm by means of communication was debated on 14 June 2022.  However, the Government indicated its intention to give full effect to the Law Commission recommendation in separate legislation, and the amendment was withdrawn.Any proposed new offence on this issue in Northern Ireland would need to be considered in the context of the Department of Health led strategy “Protect Life 2” focused on reducing suicides and the incidence of self-harm. It is important to note that Criminal matters per se are not solely the responsibility of DOJ.  Responsibility for the creation of offences has been devolved to departments; the Department with the relevant policy responsibility for a particular area within the Executive would most appropriately lead for the purposes of developing measures, criminal or otherwise, to address any offending behaviour.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on (i) her Department's discussions with the Department for Culture, Media and Sport in relation to the Online Safety Bill; and (ii) what work is being undertaken by her Department in relation to the Online Safety Bill, including the work of any departmental reference groups established to look at the Bill.
answer_text: Internet law and regulation is a reserved policy matter and the UK Government Department for Digital, Culture, Media & Sport (DCMS) is the lead department for the Online Safety Bill.  As online safety issues have the potential to impact on devolved matters, including in respect of criminal justice, I have been corresponding with DCMS Ministers and Home Office Ministers over recent months and my officials have been working with DCMS officials for some time on various aspects of the Bill.  I am supportive of the Bill in principle and endorse the need for new laws to require all services in scope to proactively remove and prevent users from being exposed to harmful and illegal content.  It is essential that companies in scope have clear and accessible ways for users to report harmful content and must be required to act quickly to take down illegal material reported to them.Through my engagement with UKG Ministers, I have sought to influence the content of the Bill where I consider it should be strengthened; this is particularly in relation to online anonymity. I have highlighted to DCMS and Home Office Ministers that verification of users is a vital component in responding to online abuse and misinformation.My officials continue to work with their DCMS colleagues on the new communications offences included in the Bill, which are intended to strengthen protections from harmful online behaviours. Officials have also been engaging on the designation of priority illegal content, proposed repeals of existing communication offences and more generally on other aspects of the Bill to prevent any gaps in or unintended impact on the Northern Ireland legislative framework.At official level, a cross-departmental reference group chaired by my Department was established to ensure that Northern Ireland Departments are alert to the implications of the Bill in terms of relevant legislation, policy and practice in Northern Ireland.  The group provides a forum for discussion on issues of shared interest or concern relating to the practical application of provisions in the Bill. Any form of abuse is unacceptable. Due to the worrying levels of online and physical abuse and harassment experienced by some election candidates and canvassers during the recent NI Assembly election campaign, you may be aware that I have recently written to party leaders and Assembly colleagues on this issue.  I have invited views regarding these experiences, which I will ensure are shared with the UK Government.  I also want to ensure that the experiences of those who had to endure these incidents are captured with a view to considering what practical measures can be taken to address what happened and prevent further incidents in the future.I remain fully committed to working collaboratively with the UK Government as this legislation progresses.
